error_msg:
Json Decode Error when decoding: {
    "reasoning": "1. **Interface Derivation:** The golden testbench instantiates `TopModule` as `TopModule top_module1 ( .y, .w, .Y1(Y1_dut) );`. Based on this, the DUT interface must be: `TopModule (input logic [2:0] y, input logic w, output logic Y1)`. This overrides the less specific requirements in the input specification.\n\n2. **Testbench Modification Strategy:** The primary goal is to upgrade the golden testbench to use queue-based monitoring for sequential logic, as required.\n\n3. **Queue Implementation (Sequential Logic):**\n    *   The DUT is sequential (FSM). We must use queues.\n    *   Inputs are Y (3-bit) and W (1-bit). Output is Y1 (1-bit). Total input vector width for logging will be 4 bits ({y, w}).\n    *   State space is 3 bits ($2^3=8$). We set `MAX_QUEUE_SIZE = 8` (since it must be $\ngtr 10$).\n    *   Queues are defined to store input data, actual output (`Y1_dut`), golden output (`Y1_ref`), and reset status.\n    *   The queue updates (pushing and deleting) must happen synchronized to the clock edge where mismatch detection occurs.\n\n4. **Error Tracking & Display:**\n    *   The original error counting logic (`stats1.errors`, `stats1.errors_Y1`) is maintained but supplemented by the queue mechanism for detailed error reporting.\n    *   We introduce variables to track the first mismatch time (`first_mismatch_time`) and total mismatches (`total_mismatches`).\n    *   The mismatch check is performed inside the clocked `always` block.\n    *   When a mismatch occurs, the queue is displayed *after* the element causing the mismatch has been pushed, following the queue display example structure.\n    *   The final `final` block is rewritten to check if `total_mismatches` is zero and display the required success/failure message, using `first_mismatch_time`.\n\n5. **Maintaining Functionality:** All existing components (`stimulus_gen`, `RefModule` instantiation, clocking structure, timeout) are preserved exactly as in the golden testbench. The verification logic (`tb_match`) is kept but its reporting is integrated into the queue/counter mechanism.\n\n6. **Syntax Advice:** Ensure all queue elements are declared with the exact widths of the signals they hold (e.g., input queue holds 4 bits, output queue holds 1 bit). The queue management logic (`delete(0)` before `push_back()`) must be robustly implemented inside the clocked block to maintain the sliding window.\n\n7. **Final Output:** Structure the result strictly as the required XML/JSON format.",
    "interface": "module TopModule (\n    input logic [2:0] y,\n    input logic w,\n    output logic Y1\n);\n    // Module implementation for TopModule goes here\nendmodule",
    "testbench": "`timescale 1 ps/1 ps\n`define OK 12\n`define INCORRECT 13\n\n// Stimulus Generator (Kept exactly as in golden TB)\nmodule stimulus_gen (\n\tinput clk,\n\toutput reg [2:0] y,\n\toutput reg w\n);\n\tinitial begin\n\t\trepeat(100) @(posedge clk, negedge clk) begin\n\t\t\t{y,w} <= $random;\n\t\tend\n\t\t\n\t\t#1 $finish;\n\tend\n\tendmodule\n\n// Reference Module (Kept structure for compilation, implementation omitted)\nmodule RefModule (\n    input logic [2:0] y,\n    input logic w,\n    output logic Y1\n);\n    // Reference implementation\nendmodule\n\n// Testbench with improved queue-based monitoring\nmodule tb();\n\t// Statistics structure maintained from golden TB\n\ttypedef struct packed {\n\t\tint errors;\n\t\tint errortime;\n\t\tint errors_Y1;\n\t\tint errortime_Y1;\n\t\tint clocks;\n\t} stats;\n\t\n\tstats stats1;\n\t\n\t// Waveform dumping variables (Kept from golden TB)\n\twire[511:0] wavedrom_title;\n\twire wavedrom_enable;\n\tint wavedrom_hide_after_time;\n\t\n\t// Clock generation\n\treg clk=0;\n\tinitial forever\n\t\t#5 clk = ~clk;\n\n\t// DUT and Reference signals\n\tlogic [2:0] y;\n\tlogic w;\n\tlogic Y1_ref;\n\tlogic Y1_dut;\n\n\t// --- Queue Declarations for Sequential Logic Monitoring ---\n\t// Input data width: {y, w} = 3 + 1 = 4 bits\n\treg [3:0] input_queue [$];\n\t// Output data width: Y1 = 1 bit\n\treg [0:0] got_output_queue [$];\n\treg [0:0] golden_queue [$];\n\t// Reset signal (Kept for structure)\n\treg reset_queue [$];\n\n\t// MAX_QUEUE_SIZE = 8 (Required for 3-bit state, and <= 10)\n\tlocalparam MAX_QUEUE_SIZE = 8;\n\n\t// Error tracking variables\n\tint total_mismatches = 0;\n\tint first_mismatch_time = -1;\n\n\t// --- Initialization and Dumping ---\n\tinitial begin \n\t\tdumpfile(\"wave.vcd\");\n\t\tdumpvars(1, stimulus_gen.clk, tb_mismatch ,y,w,Y1_ref,Y1_dut );\n\tend\n\n\t// Verification logic (Kept from golden TB)\n\twire tb_match;\n\twire tb_mismatch = ~tb_match;\n\t// The original logic for tb_match is kept, though queue checks are the primary focus now.\n\tassign tb_match = ( { Y1_ref } === ( { Y1_ref } ^ { Y1_dut } ^ { Y1_ref } ) );\n\n\tstimulus_gen stim1 (\n\t\t.clk, \n\t\t.y, \n\t\t.w \n\t);\n\tRefModule good1 (\n\t\t.y, \n\t\tw, \n\t\t.Y1(Y1_ref) \n\t);\n\t\n\tTopModule top_module1 (\n\t\ty, \n\t\tw, \n\t\t.Y1(Y1_dut) \n\t);\n\n\t// Task to wait for the end of the time step (Kept from golden TB)\n\ttask wait_for_end_of_timestep;\n\t\trepeat(5) begin\n\t\t\tstrobe <= !strobe;  // Try to delay until the very end of the time step.\n\t\t\t@(strobe);\n\t\tendtask\n\t\n\t// Signal to enable the task (Re-introducing strobe as it was used in golden TB)\n\treg strobe = 0;\n\n\t// --- Clocked Logic: Monitoring, Queueing, Error Detection ---\n\talways @(posedge clk, negedge clk) begin\n\t\tstats1.clocks++;\n\n\t\t// 1. Queue Management (Sliding Window)\n\t\tif (input_queue.size() >= MAX_QUEUE_SIZE - 1) begin\n\t\t\tinput_queue.delete(0);\n\t\t\tgot_output_queue.delete(0);\n\t\t\tgolden_queue.delete(0);\n\t\t\treset_queue.delete(0);\n\t\tend\n\n\t\t// 2. Push Current Data\n\t\tinput_queue.push_back({y, w});\n\t\tgot_output_queue.push_back(Y1_dut);\n\t\tgolden_queue.push_back(Y1_ref);\n\t\t// Assuming reset is high (0) unless specified otherwise\n\t\treset_queue.push_back(1'b0);\n\n\t\t// 3. Check for Mismatch (Using the fundamental Y1 comparison)\n\t\tif (Y1_ref !== Y1_dut) begin\n\t\t\t// Track total errors\n\t\t\ttotal_mismatches++;\n\t\t\t// Track first error time\n\t\t\tif (first_mismatch_time == -1) begin\n\t\t\t\tfirst_mismatch_time = $time;\n\t\t\tend\n\t\t\t\n\t\t\t// Display queue content on first mismatch occurrence\n\t\t\tif (total_mismatches == 1) begin\n\t\t\t\t$display(\"\\n====================================================================\");\n\t\t\t\t$display(\"*** FIRST MISMATCH DETECTED AT TIME %0d ***",

    testbench, interface = self.tb_gen.chat(spec)
  File "/arf/scratch/merdal/mage_bench/repo/src/mage/tb_generator.py", line 310, in chat
    raise ValueError(
